fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Ohio Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to ohio, speech-language pathologist compact ohio, ohio speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Ohio Speech and Hearing Professionals Board (SHPB)
board.mailing_address:
Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
77 S. High St., 16th Floor
Columbus, Ohio 43215-6108
board.physical_address: 77 S. High St., Suite 1659, Columbus, OH 43215

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Ohio. Requires a master's degree, completed clinical fellowship, passing Praxis score, and background check.

license_types.title: Conditional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their clinical fellowship (postgraduate professional experience). Application and license fee: $210. Allows supervised practice for 9 months full-time or 18 months part-time.

steps.title: Create an Ohio eLicense Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Ohio eLicense portal (elicense.ohio.gov) if you are a first-time applicant. You will create a profile and establish your login credentials.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Ohio eLicense portal and submit the application for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure. Pay the required application fee of $125 through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology to be sent directly from your academic institution to the Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Verify Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Ensure your supervisor submits the completed Verification of Supervised Professional Experience form directly to the Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Submit Praxis Scores
steps.description: Request the Educational Testing Service (ETS) to send your official Praxis Exam in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5150) scores directly to the Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Await Application Review
steps.description: The Board will review your complete application and all supporting documents. Processing typically takes 2 to 3 weeks once all materials are received and verified.

documents.detail: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
Documentation of completed 9-month full-time or 18-month part-time supervised professional experience.
Criminal background check and passport-style headshot photograph.

telepractice_text: Ohio permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Ohio SLP license to provide services to clients located in Ohio. Telepractice services must comply with Ohio's telehealth regulations and meet the same standard of care as in-person services.
provisional_text: Ohio issues a Conditional SLP License for graduates completing their clinical fellowship. The conditional license costs $210 and allows supervised practice for 9 months full-time or 18 months part-time. The supervising SLP must hold a current Ohio license and meet the Board's supervision requirements.
reciprocity_text: Ohio does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Ohio license and meet all state-specific requirements. Applicants holding the ASHA CCC-SLP may qualify for the endorsement pathway. CE hours are tracked through CE Broker.

faqs.question: How much does an Ohio SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial SLP license application fee is $200, plus an additional $3.50 eLicense Ohio maintenance fee. A Conditional SLP License for the clinical fellowship costs $210. Biennial renewal is $120.

faqs.question: How many CE hours are required for Ohio SLP renewal?
faqs.answer: Ohio requires 20 hours of continuing education every two years, with at least 2 hours in ethics and 10 hours directly related to clinical speech-language pathology practice. CE hours for the first renewal period are not required.

faqs.question: When does my Ohio SLP license expire?
faqs.answer: Ohio SLP licenses for the 2025-2026 biennium are valid until December 31, 2026. Renewal notifications are sent via email starting October of the expiration year.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Ohio participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Ohio without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Ohio?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Ohio does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Ohio license and meet all state-specific requirements. Applicants holding the ASHA CCC-SLP may qualify for the endorsement pathway. CE hours are tracked through CE Broker.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Ohio

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Ohio eLicense portal (elicense.ohio.gov), click 'Register a New Account' if you are a first-time applicant, and follow the prompts to create your profile by providing personal information and setting up login credentials.
